summary: Matched all Zelda fighter functions according to the PR body: 109 functions plus sdata2, totaling 11.4 KB / 0x2CC0 bytes, with DOL build/match status OK. The PR replaced most of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftzelda.s with C sources under src/melee/ft/chara/ftZelda, updated Zelda and Sheik headers/source references, adjusted obj_files.mk, and renamed unresolved or cross-file Zelda symbols from generic func_8013xxxx names toward ftZelda_8013xxxx names. Review focused on repository hygiene: matched functions should not remain in include/functions.h, and an unmatched Sheik-side helper that calls Zelda code should be kept as an extern placeholder until Sheik is matched.
what_changed: Moved Zelda fighter implementation out of assembly into C across ftzelda2.c, ftzelda3.c, ftzelda4.c, and ftzelda5.c, with small updates to ftzelda1.c.
Reduced asm/melee/ft/chara/ftzelda.s from thousands of lines to a much smaller residual assembly file, indicating most Zelda functions in that file were matched.
Added or expanded declarations and shared Zelda data in src/melee/ft/chara/ftZelda/ftzelda.h.
Updated obj_files.mk so the new Zelda C object files participate in the build.

Renamed a set of Zelda functions from generic func_8013xxxx symbols to ftZelda_8013xxxx symbols, including examples from review such as ftZelda_801396E0, ftZelda_80139B44, ftZelda_80139BB0, ftZelda_8013A058, ftZelda_8013A244, and ftZelda_8013B4D8.
Adjusted Sheik-side code/assembly references where Sheik interacts with Zelda transformation code, notably lbl_8011412C referencing ftZelda_8013B4D8 instead of func_8013B4D8.
Touched item assembly asm/melee/it/code_8027CF30.s with small symbol/reference updates, but the provided evidence does not establish the semantic reason beyond PR-wide symbol/name changes.
The PR body states all Zelda fighter functions matched: 109 functions plus sdata2, totaling 11.4 KB / 0x2CC0 bytes.
smart_moves: Split the matched Zelda work across multiple ftzeldaN.c translation units instead of creating one monolithic C file, following the existing character-source organization.
Kept cross-character interaction with Sheik explicit: Sheik assembly/source references were updated to point at the renamed Zelda callback symbol ftZelda_8013B4D8.
Used character-prefixed symbol names for newly matched Zelda functions, making ownership clearer than generic func_8013xxxx labels.
Preserved unmatched Sheik code as assembly/placeholder rather than forcing unrelated Sheik matching into a Zelda PR.
Included matching-status evidence in the PR body: DOL build/match status OK, function count, sdata2 mention, and byte total.
decomp_lessons: When a fighter's functions are matched, remove their provisional declarations from include/functions.h rather than merely renaming commented placeholders there.
For cross-character calls into code that is now matched, update the callsite symbol name even if the caller's owning fighter is still mostly unmatched.

If an unmatched function in another fighter must remain for linkage or documentation, use an extern placeholder until that fighter is matched; do not leave a commented-out function body as if it were active implementation.
Character-specific prefixes such as ftZelda_ are preferred over anonymous func_ labels once function ownership is known.
Large fighter decomp PRs should update build object lists, local character headers, residual asm, and any cross-file assembly references together to keep the DOL matching.

Evidence for exact function semantics is limited in the slice; avoid inferring move names or behavior beyond visible symbols such as ftZelda_SpecialHi_StartAction and ftZelda_SpecialAirHi_StartAction.
naming_conventions: Matched Zelda-owned functions should use the ftZelda_8013xxxx naming pattern rather than generic func_8013xxxx names.
Existing action entry point names such as ftZelda_SpecialHi_StartAction and ftZelda_SpecialAirHi_StartAction are retained for known fighter actions.
Unmatched labels may remain as lbl_801xxxxx, especially in still-unmatched Sheik assembly/source placeholders.
Sheik files use the project spelling ftSeak/ftseak in paths and symbols, as shown by src/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftseak.c and ftSeak_SpecialLw_StartAction.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace matched assembly blocks in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftzelda.s with C implementations while leaving only unresolved residual assembly behind.
Update assembly relocations/calls to renamed C symbols, e.g. changing references from func_8013B4D8 to ftZelda_8013B4D8 in ftseak.s.
Use sdata2 matching alongside text matching for fighter work; the PR explicitly counted '109 functions + sdata2'.

Keep callback assignments aligned across C and asm; the review excerpt shows lbl_8011412C clears fighter->cb.x21BC_callback_Accessory4 and passes ftZelda_8013B4D8 to func_8007EFC8, although the reviewer suggested keeping this as an extern placeholder until Sheik is matched.
Update obj_files.mk in the same PR as new C translation units so the matched code replaces the corresponding assembly in the build.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Matched functions should be removed from functions.h. The review suggested deleting the block rather than keeping renamed commented placeholders for matched Zelda functions.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The Sheik helper lbl_8011412C can be added as an extern placeholder until Sheik is matched, instead of adding only a commented-out C body.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Merge conflicts were resolved in DRGN-DRC#2.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that removed the reviewed Zelda placeholders from include/functions.h.
Find the PR that matches Sheik lbl_8011412C or replaces its extern placeholder.
Search for subsequent renames of ftZelda_8013xxxx symbols to semantic action/callback names.
Inspect later ftZelda header changes for struct or special-attributes naming refinements.
Search for follow-up fixes touching asm/melee/it/code_8027CF30.s after Zelda symbol renaming.
